summary: Iteration 6: Verified the recent crash-fix (autoTempo wiring to detectTrackBpm) and full PWA configuration. Frontend: app loads cleanly with NO JS / pageerror events, title is 'RIBA - Bantu Digital Audio Workstation', <link rel=manifest> + theme-color #a820ff present in <head>. Event > 'Automatic Tempo Detection' and AudioSuite > 'Reverse Audio' menu items are visible, clickable, and produce a status message ('Select an audio track first' / no-op) WITHOUT crashing the Daw component. PWA static assets (/manifest.json, /service-worker.js, /icon-192.png, /icon-512.png, /apple-touch-icon.png) all return 200 with correct content types. Backend regression: 16/16 pytest cases PASS — /api/quantize/styles returns the 5 expected ids (asiko_wisdom, makossa_roots, bikutsi_44, bikutsi_68, bikutsi_1224) and POST /api/quantize/bantu-grid {bikutsi_44, density:16, bars:4} returns 16 time_stamps_beats. No new bugs.
critical_code_review_comments: Daw.jsx is now ~2555 lines (was 2488 in iter 5, 2290 in iter 4). It keeps growing; splitting MenuBar (lines 2293-2555), the BantuModal, SetupModal, Timeline and the PWA-install useEffect into dedicated files under /app/frontend/src/components/daw/ is overdue.

PWA install handler (line 71-80) calls setStatusMsg before it's declared in source order — works at runtime (closure) but defeats lint hoisting checks; consider declaring setStatusMsg state above the install useEffect for readability.

manifest.json lang is 'fr-FR' while the public index.html still declares <html lang="en"> (line 2). Inconsistent — pick one (the UI is mostly English with French dialog labels). Recommend either lang=fr-FR in html OR lang=en in manifest.

service-worker.js is unregistered in dev mode (good) but still registered globally on every prod load — no version bump strategy is visible in the SW (no CACHE_VERSION constant grep'd). When the bundle changes, users on existing PWA installs may stay on stale assets until they manually clear. Recommend adding a versioned cache key + skipWaiting/clients.claim pattern.

Carry-over from iter 5: Bantu 'no selected track' messages are inconsistent across features ('Select a MIDI track to apply Bantu Grid' vs 'Select a MIDI track first' vs 'Select an audio track first'). Standardize.
Carry-over from iter 5: _build_bantu_grid bikutsi_44 swing slice `swing[:density % 8 or 8]` is suspicious for density not multiple of 8.
Carry-over: Modal Escape-to-close still not implemented.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (page load + 2 new menu items + PWA meta + 5 static assets)

seed_data_creation: None new this iteration (test_riba_api still creates/deletes TEST_session_1 transiently).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All targets of the user's French review request pass: (1) Daw.jsx no longer crashes — autoTempo handler (Daw.jsx:1441-1445) calls detectTrackBpm(t.id) when a track is selected, else setStatusMsg('Select an audio track first'). (2) asReverse menu item (Daw.jsx:1423) calls audioSuiteProcess('reverse'). (3) MenuBar definitions confirmed at Daw.jsx:2324 (event_auto_tempo) and 2331 (as_reverse). (4) PWA fully wired: manifest.json valid with 3 icons (192/512/180), service-worker.js served as application/javascript (registered prod-only via serviceWorkerRegistration.js), all icons reachable. (5) data-testid='install-pwa-btn' exists in Daw.jsx:1620 — does not render in headless Chromium because beforeinstallprompt isn't fired (expected per user note). Backend quantize endpoints intact. No retest required — main agent can self-verify any further tweaks.
rca of the issue: Previous white-screen crash root cause (per agent-to-agent note): MenuBar referenced action key 'autoTempo' but the action mapping in Daw.jsx did not provide such a handler, so clicking the menu attempted to invoke `undefined()` and React then re-rendered through an error. Fix in current code: handler explicitly defined at Daw.jsx:1441-1445 binding to existing detectTrackBpm useCallback (line 1247). Verified by Playwright: clicking Event > Automatic Tempo Detection with no selected track only sets statusMsg='Select an audio track first' — no pageerror, no console error, RIBA brand still visible. Issue RESOLVED.
